perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees that, during the Term and for a period of [***] years thereafter, such Party and its Affiliates will keep confidential and will not publish or otherwise disclose and will not use for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement (which includes the exercise of any rights or the performance of any obligations hereunder) any Confidential Information furnished to it or its Affiliates by the other Party or its Affiliates pursuant to this Agreement, except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or as otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties; provided, however, that the confidentiality and non-use obligations imposed by this Agreement with respect to trade secrets included in an item of Confidential Information will continue for as long as the disclosing Party continues to treat such Confidential Information as a trade secret.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that, during the Term, such Party and its Affiliates will keep confidential and will not publish or otherwise disclose and will not use for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement (which includes the exercise of any rights or the performance of any obligations hereunder) any Confidential Information furnished to it or its Affiliates by the other Party or its Affiliates pursuant to this Agreement, except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or as otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties; provided, however, that the confidentiality and non-use obligations imposed by this Agreement with respect to trade secrets included in an item of Confidential Information will continue for a period of one year after the term or as long as the disclosing Party continues to treat such Confidential Information as a trade secret, whichever is shorter.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations. The original text states that the general confidentiality obligation lasts for a period of [***] years after the term, while the obligation for trade secrets lasts as long as the information is treated as a trade secret. The modified text states that general confidentiality obligation lasts only for the term and the confidentiality and non-use obligations imposed by this Agreement with respect to trade secrets included in an item of Confidential Information will continue for a period of one year after the term or as long as the disclosing Party continues to treat such Confidential Information as a trade secret, whichever is shorter. This contradicts the implied longer period of protection for trade secrets, creating ambiguity about the actual duration of protection for confidential information, since if treated as trade secret it will be kept for a year after the term. This contradiction will affect the later interpretation of the information being trade secret and in what cases to consider it as such.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that, during the Term, such Party and its Affiliates will keep confidential and will not publish or otherwise disclose and will not use for any purpose other than as provided for in this Agreement (which includes the exercise of any rights or the performance of any obligations hereunder) any Confidential Information furnished to it or its Affiliates by the other Party or its Affiliates pursuant to this Agreement, except to the extent expressly authorized by this Agreement or as otherwise agreed to in writing by the Parties; provided, however, that the confidentiality and non-use obligations imposed by this Agreement with respect to trade secrets included in an item of Confidential Information will continue for a period of one year after the term or as long as the disclosing Party continues to treat such Confidential Information as a trade secret, whichever is shorter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the duration of confidentiality for trade secrets. The original text implies perpetual protection as long as the disclosing party treats the information as a trade secret. The modified text limits this protection to the shorter of one year after the term or as long as the disclosing party treats it as a trade secret. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity regarding the duration of trade secret protection, which falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: MMT and SIGA shall each, at their sole cost and expense, procure and maintain (a) commercial general liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] per incident and $[***] annual aggregate, and (c) product liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] annual aggregate, and each naming the other Party as additional insured.
perturbation.changed_text: MMT shall, at its sole cost and expense, procure and maintain (a) commercial general liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] per incident and $[***] annual aggregate, and (c) product liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] annual aggregate, and each naming SIGA as additional insured. SIGA shall procure and maintain product liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] annual aggregate, naming MMT as an insured.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding insurance obligations. The original text states MMT and SIGA shall each procure both commercial general liability and product liability insurance and name each other as additional insured. The modified version alters the obligation such that MMT is responsible for commercial general liability insurance only and SIGA is only responsible for product liability insurance. This creates an internal contradiction as previously both parties were assumed to have both types of insurance. This change contradicts the initial insurance requirements, affecting risk allocation and liability coverage. The contradiction appears in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MMT shall, at its sole cost and expense, procure and maintain (a) commercial general liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] per incident and $[***] annual aggregate, and (c) product liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] annual aggregate, and each naming SIGA as additional insured. SIGA shall procure and maintain product liability insurance in amounts not less than $[***] annual aggregate, naming MMT as an insured.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the insurance obligations of MMT and SIGA. The original text requires both parties to procure both commercial general liability and product liability insurance, naming each other as additional insured. The changed text alters this by making MMT responsible for commercial general liability and SIGA responsible for product liability only. This is a direct contradiction as the prior understanding was that both parties had both types of insurance. The contradiction appears in the same section.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the provisions contained in this Agreement will be severable, and the unenforceability of one will not affect the enforceability of any others or of the remainder of this Agreement. If any one or more of the provisions of this Agreement, or the application thereof in any circumstances, is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect for any reason, the Parties will negotiate in good faith with a view to the substitution therefor of a suitable and equitable solution to carry out, so far as may be valid and enforceable, the intent and purpose of such invalid provision; provided, however, that the validity, legality and enforceability of any such provision in every other respect and of the remaining provisions of this Agreement will not be in any way impaired thereby, it being intended that all of the rights and privileges of the Parties hereto will be enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by Law.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the provisions contained in this Agreement will be severable, and the unenforceability of one will not affect the enforceability of any others or of the remainder of this Agreement, unless the unenforceable provision is a material term. If any one or more of the provisions of this Agreement, or the application thereof in any circumstances, is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect for any reason, the entire agreement shall be deemed void, and the Parties will have no further obligations to each other.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in the severability clause. The original clause states that the unenforceability of one provision will not affect the enforceability of others. The modified clause states that if any provision is deemed unenforceable the entire agreement is void, and the parties have no obligations to each other. This creates contradiction because initially one provision not being enforced, doesn't affect the agreement but later, in the modified version, if one provision is held to be invalid or illegal then the agreement is automatically void. This introduces significant ambiguity and legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the provisions contained in this Agreement will be severable, and the unenforceability of one will not affect the enforceability of any others or of the remainder of this Agreement, unless the unenforceable provision is a material term. If any one or more of the provisions of this Agreement, or the application thereof in any circumstances, is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable in any respect for any reason, the entire agreement shall be deemed void, and the Parties will have no further obligations to each other.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a clear in-text contradiction within the severability clause. Initially, the agreement ensures that if one provision is unenforceable, the rest of the agreement remains valid. However, the changed text stipulates that if any provision is deemed unenforceable, the entire agreement becomes void. This directly contradicts the initial severability clause, introducing ambiguity and legal uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the agreement, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
